description_long: This object is a vintage radio receiver with a separate speaker unit, both encased in rectangular metal housings with a weathered, industrial appearance. The main unit features a large glass-covered frequency display, several rotary dials, and push buttons on its front panel, all showing signs of age and use. The speaker unit, positioned to the right of the main body, has a fabric-covered grille with a subtle logo in the upper left corner. The back of both units displays ribbed metal panels for heat dissipation, as well as visible ports and a connecting cable between the two components. The surfaces are predominantly gray with patches of rust, grime, and faded paint, suggesting long-term exposure to urban environments. This radio receiver would likely be found in garages, workshops, or as a nostalgic element in urban public spaces.
description: A weathered, vintage radio receiver with a separate speaker, featuring a glass frequency display, multiple dials, and a fabric speaker grille, all housed in aged metal casings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main radio unit with its glass frequency display, multiple dials, and the fabric-covered speaker grille with a small logo.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of both the main unit and speaker, with visible wear and a connecting cable at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying ribbed metal panels, connection ports, and the cable linking the radio and speaker units.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the plain, worn metal surfaces of both units without visible controls or grilles.
